plainEnglish: A control's accessible name must include the visible text label users see. If a button shows 'Search' but its aria-label is 'Submit', speech-input users who say 'click Search' can't activate it. Keep the visible text and the accessible name in sync.
whyItMatters: Speech-input users say a control's visible label to activate it; if the accessible name doesn't contain that visible text, the voice command fails.
quickCheck: Compare each control's visible text to its accessible name and confirm the visible text is included; axe's label-content-name-mismatch rule flags mismatches.
commonMistakes: An aria-label that replaces the visible text with different wording (axe label-content-name-mismatch).
A button showing 'Search' but named 'Submit' or 'Go' via aria-label.
Icon-plus-text buttons whose aria-label omits the visible word.
Visible label text that isn't part of the computed accessible name.

codeExamples.label: Accessible name contains the visible text
codeExamples.code:
{/* Name "Search products" starts with the visible "Search" */}
<button aria-label="Search products">Search</button>

codeExamples.label: Accessible name doesn't match visible text
codeExamples.code:
{/* Visible text says "Search" but the name is "Submit" */}
<button aria-label="Submit">Search</button>
